title: API Wrapper Tool
constraints: The tool must wrap the GitHub Users API endpoint
All HTTP errors must be caught and returned as readable messages
The agent must use the tool instead of raw API calls
No changes to the agent's core logic besides adding the tool
hints: Define a function that takes a username, calls the GitHub API, and returns structured user info
Wrap the function with the framework's tool decorator so the agent can discover and call it
Handle non-200 status codes inside the tool so the agent receives a clear error string instead of a traceback

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
import requests

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a helpful assistant that looks up GitHub user profiles."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
    ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"),
])

# BUG: The agent has no tool — it tries to answer from memory or makes raw API calls inline
# TODO: Wrap the GitHub user lookup into a proper tool with error handling

def lookup_github_user(username: str) -> str:
    response = requests.get(f"https://api.github.com/users/{username}")
    data = response.json()
    return str(data)

agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, [], prompt)
executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=[])

result = executor.invoke({"input": "Tell me about the GitHub user 'octocat'"})
print(result["output"])
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner
import requests

# BUG: This function is not registered as a tool — the agent cannot call it
# TODO: Decorate as a tool, add error handling for missing users / network failures

def lookup_github_user(username: str) -> str:
    response = requests.get(f"https://api.github.com/users/{username}")
    data = response.json()
    return str(data)

agent = Agent(
    name="GitHub Lookup Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant that looks up GitHub user profiles.",
    tools=[],
)

result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "Tell me about the GitHub user 'octocat'")
print(result.final_output)
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM
import requests

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: This function is not registered as a tool — the agent cannot call it
# TODO: Decorate as a tool, add error handling for missing users / network failures

def lookup_github_user(username: str) -> str:
    response = requests.get(f"https://api.github.com/users/{username}")
    data = response.json()
    return str(data)

agent = Agent(
    role="GitHub Lookup Assistant",
    goal="Look up GitHub user profiles",
    backstory="You are a helpful assistant that looks up GitHub user profiles.",
    tools=[],
    llm=llm,
)

task = Task(description="Tell me about the GitHub user 'octocat'", expected_output="GitHub profile info", agent=agent)
crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task], process=Process.sequential)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Tool is registered with the agent
evaluationCriteria.description: The GitHub lookup function is properly decorated and passed to the agent's tool list

evaluationCriteria.label: Handles API errors gracefully
evaluationCriteria.description: Non-200 responses and network errors return a clear message instead of crashing

evaluationCriteria.label: Returns structured user info
evaluationCriteria.description: The tool returns key profile fields (name, bio, repos) in a readable format
